Authors: Jieneng Chen, Yongyi Lu, Qihang Yu, Xiangde Luo, Ehsan Adeli, 
         Yan Wang, Le Lu, Alan L. Yuille, Yuyin Zhou

Note: TransUNet is widely recognized as one of the first and most influential 
papers to integrate Transformer architecture into medical image segmentation. 
It pioneered the hybrid CNN-Transformer approach that has since become a 
dominant paradigm in the field.

SECTION 2: MATCHING PUBMED ARTICLE
================================================================================

PMID: 39096845

Title: TransUNet: Rethinking the U-Net architecture design for medical image 
       segmentation through the lens of transformers.

Journal: Medical Image Analysis

Publication Year: 2024

Authors: Chen, Mei, Li, Lu, Yu, Wei, Luo, Xie, Adeli, Wang, Lungren, Zhang, 
         Xing, Lu, Yuille, Zhou

Abstract Summary: This journal publication represents the formal peer-reviewed 
version of the original arXiv preprint. The paper presents TransUNet as a 
versatile framework that encapsulates Transformers' self-attention into two 
key modules: (1) a Transformer encoder for global context extraction, and 
(2) a Transformer decoder for refining candidate regions. The framework 
supports three configurations: Encoder-only, Decoder-only, and Encoder+Decoder, 
with both 2D and 3D implementations available.

Key Contributions:
- First major integration of Vision Transformer into U-Net architecture
- Demonstrated significant improvements in multi-organ segmentation
- Achieved 1.06% and 4.30% Dice improvement for multi-organ and pancreatic 
  tumor segmentation respectively compared to nn-UNet
- Surpassed top-1 solution in BraTS2021 challenge

================================================================================
SECTION 3: SUMMARY
================================================================================

This report identifies TransUNet (arXiv:2102.04306) as the earliest influential 
paper combining "transformer" and "segmentation" in its title for medical 
imaging applications. Originally submitted to arXiv on February 8, 2021, 
the work has since been formally published in Medical Image Analysis (2024), 
one of the premier journals in the field (PMID: 39096845).

TransUNet established the foundational approach of hybridizing CNN local 
feature extraction with Transformer global context modeling, a paradigm 
that has influenced hundreds of subsequent works in medical image segmentation.
